paper_title: Improving Stability of Fine-Tuning Pretrained Language Models via Component-Wise Gradient Norm Clipping

problem_statement.main_issue: Pretrained Language Models (PLMs) may lose their rich domain-agnostic knowledge during fine-tuning
problem_statement.related_problem: Catastrophic forgetting problem in top layers
problem_statement.cause: Different components (feed-forward networks at different layers, fully connected matrices and biases at output layer) converge at varying speeds

previous_approach.description: Fine-tuning from top to bottom layer by layer, unfreezing new layers only when layers above have been fine-tuned

limitations.observation: Components in upper layers that have converged to local optima cannot easily be fine-tuned with newly unfrozen parameters
limitations.evidence: Parameter updates for some layers become too small to be effective

proposed_solution.basis: Component-wise convergence speed divergences is the key challenge in fine-tuning stability

evaluation_metrics.parameter_updates: Component-wise maximum rooted mean squared difference for each layer

title: Improving Stability of Fine-Tuning Pretrained Language Models via Component-Wise Gradient Norm Clipping
